cases.title: Clear Charge — Defender Set
cases.description: The defender establishes legal guarding position with both feet set and torso square before the ball handler drives into them.
evidence: Defender appears stationary with both feet set before contact.
Contact occurs through the defender's torso/chest area.
Offensive player drives directly into the set defender.
Defender is outside the restricted area.
limitations: Restricted area line is not fully visible in the frame.

cases.title: Clear Blocking Foul — Defender Still Moving
cases.description: The defender is still sliding laterally into the offensive player's path at the moment of contact.
evidence: Defender's feet are visibly in motion at the moment of contact.
Defender is sliding to the left as contact occurs.
Contact is initiated by the defender's hip, not the torso.
Offensive player was driving in a straight line.
limitations: Lower body partially obscured by another player.

cases.title: No Call — Incidental Contact
cases.description: Minor shoulder-to-shoulder contact occurs during a drive but neither player gains an unfair advantage.
evidence: Contact appears minimal and incidental.
Neither player's movement is significantly affected.
Both players continue their motion after contact.
No clear advantage or disadvantage is created.
limitations: Angle makes it difficult to assess the force of contact.

cases.title: Inconclusive — Bad Camera Angle
cases.description: The camera is positioned behind the play, obscuring the defender's feet and the moment of contact.
evidence: Defender's feet are not visible from this camera angle.
The moment of contact is partially blocked by another player.
Cannot determine if the defender was set before contact.
Video quality is too low to assess body positioning.
limitations: Camera angle does not show the defender's lower body.
Key contact moment may have occurred between extracted frames.

cases.title: Close Block/Charge — Low Confidence
cases.description: The defender appears to be nearly set but may still have slight lateral movement at the moment of contact. Very close call.
evidence: Defender appears mostly set but slight foot movement is possible.
Contact occurs through the torso area.
Offensive player drives into the defender on a straight-line path.
Defender's left foot may still be sliding at impact.
limitations: Frame rate may not capture exact moment feet were set.
Borderline call — confidence is near the threshold.
